About

What fellows actually do

Over ten weeks, fellows move from onboarding to shipped code — working in small teams on real problems the department’s own WordPress infrastructure faces.

Track-based teams

Every fellow joins one of four tracks — Plugin Architecture, Editor & Block UX, Performance & Security, or Accessibility — in a group of 3–4 students led by a faculty mentor and a graduate TA.

Program structure

  1. Week 1: Paid onboarding — local environment setup, WordPress internals, and team formation
  2. Weeks 2–4: Problem scoping with your faculty mentor and first working prototype
  3. Weeks 5–8: Core build sprint with weekly code review and a Friday all-hands demo
  4. Week 9: Polish, testing, and documentation
  5. Week 10: End-of-summer showcase presenting to faculty and department staff

Faculty mentorship

Weekly 1:1s, code review, and a Friday all-hands demo keep fellows accountable and connected — mentors also help fellows scope a capstone-ready portfolio piece from their summer work.
